creator: Jasmine Hyare
source_text:
Cinnamon Raisin Cottage Cheese Bagels 🥯
Soft, chewy, and packed with protein, these homemade cinnamon raisin bagels are perfect for breakfast or meal prep. They toast beautifully and taste even better with a little butter or cream cheese.

Makes 4 bagels
Ingredients
1 1/2 cups GF 1:1 flour or all-purpose flour, plus extra for shaping 1 1/4 cups blended cottage cheese 2 teaspoons baking powder 1/4 teaspoon salt 1 to 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon 3 tablespoons light brown sugar 1/4 cup raisins 1 beaten egg for brushing the tops

Directions
1️⃣ In a large bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and brown sugar. Fold in the raisins so they’re evenly coated with the flour mixture.

2️⃣ Add the blended cottage cheese and mix until a shaggy dough forms. If it feels too sticky, sprinkle in a little extra flour, one tablespoon at a time, until it’s easy to handle.

3️⃣ Lightly flour your work surface, divide the dough into four equal pieces, roll each into a rope, and pinch the ends together to create bagel shapes.

4️⃣ Arrange on a parchment-lined baking sheet, brush with the beaten egg, and bake at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the tops are golden and the bagels are cooked through. Let cool for several minutes before slicing. They’re delicious fresh or lightly toasted.

Macros (1 of 4)
Calories: 305 | Protein: 14g | Carbs: 46g | Fat: 4.5g | Fiber: 1.5g

#glutenfree #cottagecheese #bagels
